item.title: How the Iran war will reshape our energy future
item.description: Fatih Birol, head of the International Energy Agency, has six predictions

item.title: Pershing Square makes $64-billion takeover offer for Universal Music
item.description: Bill Ackman’s company would like to list the world’s largest music label in the U.S. after merging it with its acquisition vehicle

item.title: Business Brief: Five files to follow
item.description: Trump’s deadline runs into a resolute Tehran

item.title: Italian government poised to replace Leonardo CEO who oversaw order surge
item.description: Move by Italian government comes as Roberto Cingolani’s three-year term at defence group expires next month

item.title: Citi sets aggressive targets for bankers in wealth management unit
item.description: Revised goals involve net revenue and value of assets clients entrust to Citi as business has lagged Wall Street peers

item.title: How to know when to change financial advisors
item.description: Lower fees are one reason investors switch, but investment style and fit are also factors

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing offers to buy Universal Music in €55bn deal
item.description: Proposed transaction would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

item.title: Iran remains defiant as clock ticks down on Trump’s deadline
item.description: Pakistani mediation efforts at ‘critical stage’ as Israel warns Iranians to stay away from trains and railways

item.title: Doctors’ demands could cost NHS up to £30bn a year, says Streeting
item.description: Health secretary warns that acceding to further pay rise would lead to wage claims from other staff, as latest six-day strike begins

item.title: Air India chief quits early after turbulent tenure
item.description: Campbell Wilson’s ambition to turn carrier into world-class airline marred by fatal crash of Boeing Dreamliner jet

item.title: Toronto home sales post first monthly increase since September
item.description: Region saw 4,546 home purchases last month, 1.4% higher than February, according to the TRREB

item.title: The painful lessons of the Iran War
item.description: Controlling the flow of fossil fuels and food equals power

item.title: As middle powers forge a future without the U.S., Canada walks a tricky path
item.description: Middle-power collaboration puts Canada in a delicate situation ahead of pending negotiations with the U.S. and Mexico

item.title: Don’t forget the Mag 7
item.description: Still a third of the S&P 500 — and wobbling a bit

item.title: SpaceX bankers bend over backwards to bag IPO role
item.description: To infinity back-scratching and beyond

item.title: Private equity buyouts nosedive
item.description: Plus, government bond demand booms, asset manager consolidation wave and Vincent in Brixton at the Orange Tree

item.title: Jamie Dimon angers private credit rivals . . . again
item.description: The JPMorgan chief warns that private credit losses will be larger than many expect

item.title: Top record labels and start-up Suno hit impasse in AI-generated music talks
item.description: One executive says there is ‘no path’ towards licensing deal under current proposal

item.title: Bezos’s Project Prometheus poaches xAI co-founder from OpenAI
item.description: Kyle Kosic joins Project Prometheus, the secretive start-up working on systems that can understand the physical world

item.title: The internet is deciding what to forget
item.description: Do digital bananas in Hawaiian shirts chatting up pineapples need to be saved for posterity? Probably not

item.title: Brussels banking rules could hit lenders and UK-EU ‘reset’, City figures warn
item.description: Concern that efforts to boost European defence spending could also be damaged

item.title: The future of global trade won’t depend on the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A new infrastructure is being built rapidly to avoid the strategic chokepoint and ensure global energy and food security

item.title: Europe’s fossil fuel dependence poses risks to price stability
item.description: Repeated cost shocks make a transition to cleaner energy critical

item.title: US counts cost of equipment destroyed in Iran war
item.description: Lost aircraft and radars contribute to estimated $500mn daily price tag and leave US exposed elsewhere

item.title: UK pubs and restaurants face ‘permacrisis’ after energy shock
item.description: Hospitality sector already hit by increases to business rates and wages

item.title: JPMorgan to build Canary Wharf’s tallest tower after City airport approval
item.description: Bank wins clearance for 265-metre skyscraper after talks over low-flying aircraft

item.title: China offers diplomatic immunity in bid to host oceans treaty
item.description: Beijing lobbying stepped up at UN meetings over summit for high seas

item.title: B.C. company says nine of ten abducted mine workers confirmed dead in Mexico
item.description: Mexican authorities had reported the arrests of four people believed to be tied to the disappearances

item.title: Burger King enters next phase of turnaround plan with president Tom Curtis leading the way
item.description: Chain rolls out revamped version of signature Whopper burger in Canada amid renewed marketing push

item.title: Telus boosts head count but sheds Canadian jobs as it reduces staff in core business
item.description: Telecom-related work forces have been falling in recent years as companies adopt new technology

item.title: Middle East war day 38 as it happened: Iran calls Trump’s threats against infrastructure ‘delusional’; oil prices jump
item.description: Tehran says US president’s ‘rude’ and ‘baseless’ comments do not make up for America’s ‘humiliation’ in the region

item.title: Next Competition Bureau leader must embrace Canada Strong mindset
item.description: Acting Commissioner Jeanne Pratt should quickly approve Keyera’s purchase of Canadian pipelines from Texas-based Plains

item.title: As Iran’s civilian economy crumbles, its military economy grows stronger
item.description: War is splitting the country in two

item.title: U.S. Fed official says rate hike possible if inflation remains elevated
item.description: Rate increases would be sharp shift from late last year, when Federal Reserve cut key rate three times

item.title: U.S. private equity firm closes $400-million deal for Montreal amateur sports streamer LiveBarn
item.description: The platform livestreams amateur and youth sports from arenas, courts and fields across North America

item.title: Why are these big name investing apps using the gambling playbook to lure clients?
item.description: Some are forgetting they must balance what’s good for their business with what’s good for clients

item.title: Ukraine’s drones dent Russia’s war-fuelled oil windfall
item.description: Moscow’s disrupted energy exports add pressure to markets already reeling from Iran conflict

item.title: Saudi Arabia charges record premium for its oil
item.description: World’s biggest crude exporter to ask Asian customers for around $20 a barrel on top of benchmark prices

item.title: Sales of used EVs surge in US as petrol prices pass $4 a gallon
item.description: Americans are buying second-hand electric vehicles even as the market for new vehicles slumps

item.title: Runway safety incidents on the rise in Canada, but very close calls level off
item.description: Country has seen about one high-risk incident a year on average since 2018, data show

item.title: Jamie Dimon warns private credit losses will be larger than feared
item.description: JPMorgan chief raises alarm on weakening lending standards in annual shareholder letter

item.title: An Economist’s Quest to Solve America’s Wage Problem
item.description: Arindrajit Dube argues that the answer is empowering workers and setting mandatory wage standards across industries.

item.title: Quebecor’s Freedom Mobile is enjoying a newfound status
item.description: The telecom carrier is taking advantage of a federal policy that gives it access to rivals’ networks and expires in 2030

item.title: Why doesn’t Ontario want its pension funds to manage its new investment agency?
item.description: Ontario’s taxpayers deserve answers on how the government intends to spend their money

item.title: A wave of cheap Chinese EVs is poised to upend Canada’s auto industry

item.title: As U.S. probes Canada over forced labour, data suggest it faces gaps of its own
item.description: Review of Chinese trade statistics, recent U.S. enforcement record suggest U.S. is scaling back detention of goods

item.title: Broken CN Rail bridge at Vancouver port saps energy superpower ambitions
item.description: The Second Narrows Rail Bridge plays a critical role in shipping at Canada’s biggest and busiest port

item.title: How to fight car thefts, fentanyl and lift our economy – all at once
item.description: Unlike in other countries, shipping manifest data is a black hole in Canada – the public does not know what goes in and out

item.title: US employment: more sludge
item.description: Plus, guns and butter redux

item.title: Why this oil shock is different
item.description: Governments and central banks are out of policy ammunition to contain the economic fallout

item.title: Russian crypto payment system expands into Africa
item.description: Promotional videos show office of cryptocurrency network A7 in Nigeria, while company also claims new branch in Zimbabwe

item.title: Boring stocks are still beating the herd
item.description: Index of under-reported UK equities has done better than the FTSE 100

item.title: Private equity buyouts slump as AI fears and war dent dealmaking
item.description: Groups agreed acquisitions worth $172bn in three months to March, a 36% fall from previous quarter

item.title: Power-starved Cuba deepens reliance on Chinese solar tech
item.description: Imports of the technology have soared, offering some respite for country that now faces near-total oil blockade

item.title: World’s biggest battery maker takes ambitions to the high seas
item.description: China’s CATL wants to electrify global shipping fleets but hurdles remain to large-scale adoption

item.title: The current market volatility is a good test for DIY investors
item.description: If you’re having a hard time right now you might need to reconsider your choice of managing your own accounts

item.title: Iran allows Iraqi ships through Hormuz strait
item.description: Tehran’s decision could allow 3mn b/d of oil to be released to international markets

item.description: Market Questions is the FT’s guide to the week ahead

item.title: All that glisters: Maga influencers promote gold but investors feel short-changed
item.description: Marketing pitches tell retirees the precious metal is a safe harbour amid claims of misrepresentation and exorbitant fees

item.title: Nelson Peltz’s bidding war highlights $25bn wave of asset manager consolidation
item.description: Quest for scale puts global money manager tie-ups on pace to crush last year’s deal total as costs and competition mount

item.title: Why McDonald’s and KFC are growing like wildfire in China
item.description: Western fast-food brands are expanding into the countryside

item.title: Inflation or recession? The tug of war in bond markets
item.description: Governments’ borrowing costs are being pulled in opposite directions

item.title: AI and tech companies drive Hong Kong listings to 5-year high
item.description: Primary and secondary offerings raise more than $13bn in first quarter of 2026

item.title: Campaign to curb cars in Berlin sparks uproar ahead of election
item.description: Conservatives and far right are opposing a citizens’ initiative to restrict traffic in the German capital

item.title: Countries must not hoard fuel during Iran war, warns IEA
item.description: Fatih Birol makes veiled reference to China as he urges nations to avoid export bans despite worsening supply shock
